sections.title: If The Fremennik Trials has not been completed

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Get lost, outlander! I've no time for you!

sections.title: If The Fremennik Trials has been completed

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Who is that? Identify yourself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's me, [player name]. What are you doing out here?

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Nothing... Just carving these horribly broken pieces of driftwood.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: In the middle of nowhere.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: In damp clothing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, have a nice day.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Wait! Would you do me a favour?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of a favour?

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Look, you see that heap of driftwood over there? That is my ship, and all that is left of my one shot at making my ancestors proud of me.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: My family have always been great explorers and seafarers, and I recently set off on my maiden voyage.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: However, a big storm - yes, a huge storm in fact - blew my ship onto the rocks. Three times.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: I've been sitting here ever since, trying to think of a way to make my family proud, and if you will help me...I think I have a way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll help you out.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Thank you! I don't have much apart from my carvings, some driftwood and the map that I was following when I had my little...

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: ...accident.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: If you help me out, then I'll gladly hand it over.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, what is your plan?

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Well, I have a little food left, although it's a little briny.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: If you take a couple of carvings to my wife and son in the village over the hill and tell them that I sent you with them from a distant island, then I can wait out here for a week or so before returning.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, hand me over the carvings and I'll be on my way.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Well, none of these are any good. I'll need some fresh wood from a decent tree.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: When I was swimming for my life I saw an odd-looking windswept tree to the east of here, up that cliff.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Could you bring me some of that wood so I can work it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, you must have pretty good eyes to be able to see a single tree from the water during a heavy storm.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Yes...great eyes...they run in the family.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Possibly later, when you don't smell so damp.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Bah! Well, if you aren't going to help, do me a favour and jump off that cliff!

sections.title: Talking to Olaf again without the windswept logs

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: So... Got the logs for me yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I just popped back to see how you were doing.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Still cold. Still damp. 'Bout average, really.

sections.title: Talking to Olaf with the windswept logs

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Olaf, I cut some logs from the tree. Here's the wood.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Wonderful! I'll carve them up now.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Done!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's it?

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Yep!

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: This is for my wife, Ingrid. I'm sure she will love it. Tell her it's tribal or something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure...

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: And this one is for my son, Volf. Kids love planks in my experience. Tell him I said hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. They live in Rellekka, right?

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Yes. Hurry back! And don't forget the story: I'm not here, I'm someplace far away.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: So, how did my wife like her carving?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know... I've still got it.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Well, hurry up and give it to her then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She was very happy with it!

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Wonderful!

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: I wish i could see her again...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, if this plan works out you will be able too, right?

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Yes, you're right.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Did my son like his present? I'll bet he was thrilled.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll bet he will be... but I've still got it.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Well, take your time over it, why don't you.

lines.speaker: Ingrid Hradson
lines.text: Hello again! Have you any word from my husband?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, no, not yet. It takes a while for the messages to reach me, you know.

lines.speaker: Ingrid Hradson
lines.text: Well, when you do, tell him we'll be more than happy to see him again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me, but are you Ingrid? The same Ingrid that is married to Olaf Hradson?

lines.speaker: Ingrid Hradson
lines.text: Yes I am. Why do you ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am a friend of your husband. He sent me here with a gift for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: An expensive, exotic gift from the far-flung and tropical shores of the Island of Obscurity.

lines.speaker: Ingrid Hradson
lines.text: How wonderful! I assumed from the way the big oaf always referred to the rigging as 'big strings' that he couldn't navigate his way out of a soggy paper bag. So, can I see the gift he sent?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Certainly! Here it is. He sends his best wishes along with it.

lines.speaker: Ingrid Hradson
lines.text: I see. It looks like a log with 'Olaf was here' carved in it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's tribal! Very tribal! I watched the natives carve it myself.

lines.speaker: Ingrid Hradson
lines.text: Well, since you have come such a long way, you can have this. I baked it this morning, so it's still nice and fresh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, thank you! Enjoy your Obscurian Tribal Artefact(tm). It has a million-and-one uses!

lines.speaker: Volf Olafson
lines.text: Hello again! Does my father send any word...or treasures like before?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not today, but if he does, you will be the first to know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Volf? Son of Olaf Hradson?

lines.speaker: Volf Olafson
lines.text: Yes. Do I know you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, but I do know your father. He sent me with a present for you from, err...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: From the Island of Obscurity.

lines.speaker: Volf Olafson
lines.text: Oh! That was fast! He only set off recently, and I was wondering how he was doing. So, where is this gift?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right here. Enjoy!

lines.speaker: Volf Olafson
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very valuable. Have a nice time!

lines.speaker: Volf Olafson
lines.text: Wait! Here, take this. I was going to have it for my lunch, but I suppose you can have it in payment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks! Enjoy your new...thing!

sections.title: Talking to Olaf after the presents have been delivered
Talking to Olaf again if the fire has not lighten up

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: So...very...cold. Almost cold enough to tear up an old map with my fevered shivering. Cough, cough.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Ahhhhhhhh... Much better.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm glad you're happy. Can I have that map now?

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Well, okay...but could I have a little of that food first? I've had nothing but seaweed and biscuits for the past week.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not a chance.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Okay, okay, I was only asking.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Well, regardless, you've more than earned this map. It was the last one my grandfather, Sven the Helmsman, ever made.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: It is supposed to show the location of one of his stashes of treasure, but I have never been able to decipher the strange, runic inscriptions that point the way.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: I wish you luck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, thanks. What will you do now?

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: I'll sit here and practice carving.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: If you pass this way again, I'll be glad if you drop my next load of work off with my family.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: That or bring me some food before I starve. Whichever.

sections.title: Talking to Olaf again after getting Sven's last map
Reclaiming back the map if lost

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Olaf, I seem to have lost your map, can I have another?

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: You lost the treasured map of Sven the Helmsman? How dare you!

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: You'd best take better care of this one, or else!

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Hey! I'm trying to carve and you're standing in my light! Move it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Olaf, are you all right?

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: So...cold...need...fire.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I delivered your carvings as you asked. Your family would like to see you again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here, let me get you to the village before you freeze to death.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: I won't...go...anywhere. I don't...care if I...freeze.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: I'd rather...die...than suffer...the shame.

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Just get the...fire burning. Use...these.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, at least give me the map like you promised!

lines.speaker: Olaf Hradson
lines.text: Too...cold...to...hear...whining.

sections.title: When digging beside the Windswept tree
Attempting to open the picture wall
Opening the chest inside the shipwreck

lines.speaker: Ulfric
lines.text: Slay... Outlander... Intruder!
